title: Cooperative Polygraphy

jokes.text: I guess I knew Pierce was part of a weird, futuristic cult, but I wasn't prepared for a funeral with so much beeping.
jokes.setup: Normal funeral expectations vs Pierce's bizarre cult funeral
jokes.punchline: The specific detail of 'so much beeping' at a funeral
jokes.explanation: Great economy and specificity - 'so much beeping' is an unexpected detail that makes the absurd cult funeral vivid and funny

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So much beeping at a funeral.

jokes.text: Well, let's not judge. Everyone has a right to whatever fake religion they delusionally choose.
jokes.setup: Shirley advocating tolerance and non-judgment
jokes.punchline: Immediately calling it 'fake' and 'delusional' while claiming not to judge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley character moment - her attempt at tolerance immediately undercut by her judgmental nature

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: We shouldn't judge people for their beliefs. Everyone deserves acceptance and respect, no matter how different they are from us.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I mean, that's just fake and delusional, but I'm not judging.

jokes.text: ♪ Troy and Abed are in mourning ♪
jokes.setup: Their signature jingle format at inappropriate moment
jokes.punchline: Wordplay on 'mourning/morning' during actual mourning
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay within established character dynamic, though predictable for these characters

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Good morning!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Troy, we're at a funeral.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Good mourning.

jokes.text: Will you guys please stop doing that? I can't believe you did it during your eulogy. So uncomfortable.
jokes.setup: The inappropriate timing of their jingle
jokes.punchline: The horrifying revelation they did it during the actual eulogy
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy escalation - what seemed bad gets worse with context

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Wait, you did the jingle during the eulogy?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: We didn't know when else to do it!

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: It's a funeral, Jeff. You don't perform a jingle at a funeral.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: In our defense, the timing seemed right at the moment.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Right when they were lowering the casket?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: We thought it was uplifting!

jokes.text: I don't think the audience got that we were singing 'mourning' with a 'u'. / You were singing 'mourning' with a 'u'? / Oh, no.
jokes.setup: Abed's concern about audience understanding the pun
jokes.punchline: Troy realizing he sang the wrong word entirely
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - the attempt to explain the pun reveals Troy never understood it in the first place

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Do you think the audience will get the pun?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What pun?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I've been saying 'cool. Cool cool cool' but I've been meaning to say 'cool. Cool cool cool' with a different emphasis.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wait, you've been singing 'cool cool cool'?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I've been singing 'Fool, fool, fool' the whole time.

jokes.text: Once you reach level 16, you can see the color blurble.
jokes.setup: Explaining the cult's theology with apparent seriousness
jokes.punchline: The made-up color 'blurble'
jokes.explanation: Great made-up word that sounds plausible but absurd - 'blurble' is perfectly crafted nonsense

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: The made-up color 'blurble' is actually central to their theology. It represents the space between consciousness and reality.

jokes.text: Of all the ridiculous cartoon nonsense. If there is a blurble, the Lord keeps it hidden for a reason.
jokes.setup: Shirley dismissing the cult's beliefs
jokes.punchline: Immediately incorporating 'blurble' into her own religious framework
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley moment - dismisses one belief system while inadvertently validating it through her own

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: That's ridiculous, I don't believe in any of that nonsense.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Although, you know, the Bible does mention blurble. It's right there in Deuteronomy.

jokes.text: What up, N-Bombs? How was the funeral? Awesome?
jokes.setup: Chang's inappropriate greeting and energy
jokes.punchline: Asking if a funeral was 'awesome'
jokes.explanation: Classic Chang inappropriateness but fairly predictable for his character

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Was it awesome?

jokes.text: No, Chang, our friend's funeral was not awesome. It was deeply sad. You know, funeral style.
jokes.setup: Jeff's exasperated response to Chang
jokes.punchline: The redundant clarification 'funeral style'
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery with the unnecessary qualifier adding to the exasperation

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: A funeral? Like, a funeral funeral? Funeral style?

jokes.text: All right, are you sure you guys aren't just doing the 'respect the dead' jig-a-lig?
jokes.setup: Chang questioning their sincerity about mourning Pierce
jokes.punchline: The made-up phrase 'jig-a-lig' for performative mourning
jokes.explanation: Chang's made-up terminology is character-specific and mildly clever

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: You're all doing the jig-a-lig.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What's the jig-a-lig?

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: It's that thing you do when you're pretending to be sad about something.

jokes.text: By the time Pierce was my age, he had already been fired from 15 jobs. I've only seen two Police Academies. The last two.
jokes.setup: Reflecting on living life to the fullest like Pierce
jokes.punchline: Completely unrelated confession about Police Academy movies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy logic - earnest attempt at profundity derailed by random pop culture confession

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I've been thinking about Pierce and how he lived life to the fullest, you know? Just doing whatever he wanted, whenever he wanted. I think I should be more like that.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: So I have a confession to make. I've watched all seven Police Academy movies. Not just watched them—I own them. On DVD. And I watch them constantly.

jokes.text: That's easy for you to say. And for us to say.
jokes.setup: Man introduces himself as 'Mr. Stone'
jokes.punchline: Jeff pointing out it's an unusually easy name to say
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with economic delivery, though not particularly deep

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Stone
dialogue.line: Hi, I'm Mr. Stone.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's an unusually easy name to say.

jokes.text: Aw, schnizzle. You guys are in trouble! Later.
jokes.setup: Chang's excitement about others being in trouble
jokes.punchline: Immediately trying to leave when he learns he's also a suspect
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven reversal - Chang's glee turns to immediate retreat

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Oh no, this is the best day of my life!

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Wait, I'm a suspect? I'm outta here.

jokes.text: Fine, but I ask the questions. / I'm afraid I can't allow that. / All right, then just the 'fine' part.
jokes.setup: Chang trying to negotiate control
jokes.punchline: Immediately capitulating when refused
jokes.explanation: Good quick reversal showing Chang's complete lack of backbone

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: I demand control of the study group!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Okay, fair enough.

jokes.text: We're losing Pierce! Everyone, quickly! Someone get a balloon.
jokes.setup: Medical emergency situation
jokes.punchline: The utterly inadequate response of getting a balloon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - the complete mismatch between emergency and response is perfectly crafted

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: We need to call 911!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I'm calling them right now!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. I have a better idea.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Get a balloon.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: A balloon? Someone's dying!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: A balloon always helps.

jokes.text: I always wanted to try a polygraph. They're like the pie fight of cop movies. Or the acupuncture of the legal system.
jokes.setup: Abed's media-analysis perspective on polygraphs
jokes.punchline: Two oddly specific analogies that somehow make sense
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Abed - weird but accurate pop culture analogies that reveal deeper truth

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: A polygraph is like a three-act structure for your nervous system. The setup is the control questions, the confrontation is the lie, and the resolution is when your palms get sweaty.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Or it's like a laugh track, but for guilt. It picks up on the audio cues your body's making when you're being dishonest.

jokes.text: Yeah, what a waste of murder. I mean, he was, like, 79.
jokes.setup: Discussing the implausibility of murdering Pierce
jokes.punchline: The logic that murder would be wasteful due to his age
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Troy's innocent delivery making the morbid logic even funnier

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Why would we murder Pierce? He's like 65 years old. We can just wait.

jokes.text: If I had a final wish, I'd use it to stay alive.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Pierce's final wish
jokes.punchline: The obvious but unspoken optimal use of a final wish
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with perfect economy - points out the obvious flaw in the concept

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So Pierce's final wish is... what exactly?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: He wants us to scatter his ashes at his favorite place.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: That's actually really sweet.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Or he could wish for more wishes.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's... that's not how final wishes work.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Technically, it could be.

jokes.text: In all your sexual fantasies about Pierce Hawthorne, none of them involved his murder?
jokes.setup: Routine polygraph questioning
jokes.punchline: The horrifyingly specific and inappropriate question
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy - the casual delivery of something deeply inappropriate

dialogue.speaker: Polygraph Examiner
dialogue.line: You ever cheat on a test?

dialogue.speaker: Stone
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Polygraph Examiner
dialogue.line: You ever cheat on your partner?

dialogue.speaker: Stone
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Polygraph Examiner
dialogue.line: Have you ever been sexually aroused by a member of your immediate family?

dialogue.speaker: Stone
dialogue.line: What?! No!

jokes.text: Are you gay? / No. / Are you sure you're not gay? / Yes. / Gay murderer says what? / What? / He's telling the truth.
jokes.setup: Increasingly ridiculous questions designed to trick Jeff
jokes.punchline: Jeff falling for the childish trap then being told he's gay
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed escalation with good payoff - the childish trick working on sophisticated Jeff

dialogue.speaker: Stone
dialogue.line: If a train leaves Chicago at 60 miles per hour and another train leaves Boston at 40 miles per hour, which one will reach the station first?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Well, obviously the one going 60 miles per hour.

dialogue.speaker: Stone
dialogue.line: Wrong. Neither one, because they're going in opposite directions.

dialogue.speaker: Stone
dialogue.line: Okay, here's another one. A man walks into a bar. What does he order?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: A drink?

dialogue.speaker: Stone
dialogue.line: No, he orders a drink and a woman walks in. What does she order?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I don't know, what?

dialogue.speaker: Stone
dialogue.line: That means you're gay.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What? That doesn't even make sense!

jokes.text: I've been instructed to point out that that means you're gay.
jokes.setup: The polygraph operator's professional demeanor
jokes.punchline: Delivering Pierce's juvenile insult with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between professional delivery and juvenile content

dialogue.speaker: Polygraph Operator
dialogue.line: Your butt is a dork.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: What?

jokes.text: Troy Barnes. / D'oh! I meant to say, 'butts Carlton.'
jokes.setup: Simple request to state his name
jokes.punchline: Troy's nonsensical 'correction' that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy randomness - 'butts Carlton' is memorably absurd and character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: My name is Troy. Troy Barnes.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Actually, it's Troy. T-R-O-I. No Y. Just kidding, there's a Y.

jokes.text: We may have a designated rendezvous point and a couple of bugout bags filled with gold coins and condoms just in case we need to bargain our way onto a boat.
jokes.setup: Admitting to having a doomsday plan
jokes.punchline: The specific and oddly practical details of gold coins and condoms for boat bargaining
jokes.explanation: Great specificity in the absurd planning - the condoms detail pushes it into memorable territory

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I have a doomsday plan. I've got a bunker with enough supplies for five years, a chest of gold coins, and a boat.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Why do you need a boat if you have a bunker?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: For when the bunker floods. And I've got condoms.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: For what, making water balloons?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: For bartering. Gold coins are great, but everyone wants condoms in the apocalypse. It's currency and protection.

jokes.text: The gold coins are for buying food.
jokes.setup: The implication about what the condoms are for
jokes.punchline: Abed's practical clarification that implies the worse option
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan clarification that makes the implication worse by elimination

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Well, we could use them for water balloons.

jokes.text: It's a doomsday plan, not a picnic plan. Do you really wanna see the horrors we might have to endure just to get on a boat?
jokes.setup: Friends being upset they're not included in the plan
jokes.punchline: Troy's earnest explanation of why exclusion is actually merciful
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy with Troy's innocent delivery of horrific implications

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Look, I didn't tell you guys because I was being merciful. You think I want to watch you suffer? I'm protecting you from disappointment.

jokes.text: Benjamin Franklin Chang, ready to deal out the truth. Nothing to hide. Let's do this.
jokes.setup: Chang declaring his honesty and openness
jokes.punchline: The immediate reveal of his most shameful secret
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup for the immediate reversal, though predictable for Chang

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: I'm an open book. I have nothing to hide. I'm completely honest about everything.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: I like watching cooking shows in my underwear and I cry at them.

jokes.text: Have you ever masturbated in the study room?
jokes.setup: Chang's confident declaration of having nothing to hide
jokes.punchline: The most mortifying possible first question
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy timing - hits Chang with the worst possible question after his bravado

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: I have nothing to hide. Ask me anything.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay. What's your first name?

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: ...Kevin.

jokes.text: Have you ever 9/11'd anyone?
jokes.setup: Questioning Abed with inappropriate casualness
jokes.punchline: Using '9/11' as a verb in casual conversation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that works through casual delivery, though potentially offensive

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So Abed, what happened with that girl you were talking to?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: It didn't work out.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yeah? What happened, did she 9/11 you?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: ...Did you just use 9/11 as a verb?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Too soon?

jokes.text: Do you and Troy still actively use Jeff's Netflix account without his permission? / Wait, what? / You told Pierce that?
jokes.setup: Routine questioning revealing secrets
jokes.punchline: Jeff discovering his Netflix account theft
jokes.explanation: Good revelation comedy with realistic friendship dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Wait, someone's been watching my Netflix account?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Yeah, they've been binge-watching everything. Lately it's been a lot of reality TV.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Reality TV? I don't watch reality TV.

dialogue.speaker: Stone
dialogue.line: That's because it wasn't you.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Well who was it?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: We were kind of hoping you could tell us.

jokes.text: Is that why my review of The Grey is constantly changing? / Yes, stop giving it four stars. / I like Liam Neeson! / Then send him a message about the roles he chooses.
jokes.setup: Jeff discovering the Netflix theft
jokes.punchline: Troy's earnest critique of Liam Neeson's career choices
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Troy's naive suggestion and movie criticism

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Troy, did you steal the Netflix password?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Jeff, I have to be honest with you. Liam Neeson's career peaked with Schindler's List. Everything after that has been a slow decline into straight-to-streaming action movies where he plays a guy with a very specific set of skills.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's... not actually what I asked.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I know, but someone needed to say it.

jokes.text: Is that why my review of The Grey is constantly changing?
jokes.setup: Learning about the Netflix theft
jokes.punchline: Jeff's personal mystery finally solved
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing a long-standing minor annoyance had a simple explanation

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're telling me that someone in this study group has been stealing from Netflix this whole time?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I can't believe it. After all these years, my personal mystery is finally solved.

jokes.text: I padded your share of the rent by $10 and I've been putting it into a savings account that yields 4%. You'll be thanking me in six years when you find out you have $86.
jokes.setup: Annie confessing to financial manipulation
jokes.punchline: The pathetically small return on investment after six years
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie moment - well-intentioned but misguided financial planning with realistic terrible returns

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I may have made some questionable financial decisions with the study group's funds over the years.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: How questionable?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: After six years of careful investing and strategic planning, we've made a total return of... thirty-seven cents.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Thirty-seven cents?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Pre-tax.

jokes.text: That's jacket money.
jokes.setup: Learning about the $86 in savings
jokes.punchline: Troy's specific assessment of what $86 can buy
jokes.explanation: Good economy and character-specific way of valuing money

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Eighty-six dollars? That's... that's a lot of money. I could buy 86 dollar cheeseburgers, or 172 fifty-cent tacos, or I could finally get that solid gold toilet seat I've had my eye on.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: A toilet seat isn't solid gold for eighty-six dollars.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Not with that attitude.

jokes.text: We've been washing paper plates and making our own toothpaste. But don't you worry. When we have robot bodies on the moon, we can share a free jacket.
jokes.setup: Discovering Annie's been saving their money
jokes.punchline: Escalating from poverty details to absurdist future scenario
jokes.explanation: Excellent Abed escalation from mundane to sci-fi absurd while maintaining the financial complaint

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: So Annie's been saving all our money? That's actually responsible.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Someone has to be. Do you know how much we spend on coffee?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's just survival. Without coffee, we'd have to face reality.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Exactly. Which is why I've also been rationing your meals.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: My meals? I thought I was just getting thinner from stress.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: At this rate, we'll have enough saved to buy a small island.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: An island? Annie, we can't even afford to leave the study room.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Not with that attitude. In five years, we'll be island people.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: And in ten years we'll be gods worshipped by island natives who think we control the sun.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: See? This is why I save. For the sun-god years.

jokes.text: That's a stereotype. / Was that anti-semitism? / No! That's sensitivity. It's anti-semitic to do things like that when you know full well you're Jewish.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Annie hoarding money
jokes.punchline: Shirley's convoluted logic about antisemitism
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with Shirley's well-meaning but problematic logic

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I'm not hoarding money, I'm being fiscally responsible.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: That's what the Jewish people said, and look where that got them.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: What? That doesn't make any sense.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Exactly. Antisemitism doesn't make sense either, but it's still a thing.

jokes.text: Your rating system is flawed.
jokes.setup: The ongoing Netflix argument
jokes.punchline: Troy's definitive judgment on Jeff's taste
jokes.explanation: Good callback with economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Jeff, I've been thinking about your Netflix argument, and I have to tell you something. Your taste in shows is objectively worse than everyone else's in this study group.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You watch things ironically. You watch things because they're 'guilty pleasures.' You watch things that are just bad, Jeff. And you convince yourself you're being intellectual about it.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: The rest of us? We just like what we like. But you? You're up there doing this whole performance, and it's exhausting to watch.

jokes.text: They're all lying. / We all know that, you judgmental bitch!
jokes.setup: The polygraph operator stating the obvious
jokes.punchline: Britta's explosive response to being called out
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Britta's defensive explosion

jokes.text: Are you aware that, in order to save money, Shirley stopped using real tofu and replaced it with a midwestern tofu substitute called 'meatfu'?
jokes.setup: Revealing Shirley's cost-cutting measures
jokes.punchline: The made-up word 'meatfu' for fake tofu
jokes.explanation: Great made-up word that's both funny and plausible for a Midwestern substitute

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: It's not fake tofu, it's meatfu.

jokes.text: It's still not actually meat... Legally.
jokes.setup: Defending the meatfu substitute
jokes.punchline: The qualifier 'legally' implying it might be meat in practice
jokes.explanation: Excellent qualifier that makes the situation worse through legal technicality

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: It's legally meat.

jokes.text: Well, no higher than usual. / Not true.
jokes.setup: Britta defending her drug use at the baptism
jokes.punchline: The polygraph catching her in a lie about her normal drug use
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - trying to minimize gets caught as additional lie

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I barely use drugs anymore!

dialogue.speaker: Polygraph Operator
dialogue.line: That's a lie.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Well, yeah, but not about the drugs.

jokes.text: At least with a bris there's an element of suspense.
jokes.setup: Comparing religious ceremonies for entertainment value
jokes.punchline: Finding suspense in circumcision versus baptism
jokes.explanation: Dark and clever observation about religious ceremony entertainment value

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Okay, but circumcision has way more suspense. You don't know if the guy's going to mess up.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's not the point of circumcision.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Well it should be. Baptism is just dunking. Circumcision is like a thriller.

jokes.text: Well, next time, I'll have Cheech and Chong do the warm-up!
jokes.setup: Britta's drug use at church
jokes.punchline: Shirley's sarcastic suggestion for drug-themed entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good Shirley escalation showing her anger through sarcasm

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Well, maybe we should just have a drug-themed puppet show next Sunday. I'm sure that would really bring in the youth group.

jokes.text: You told me a hawk stole them!
jokes.setup: Discovering Jeff kept her underwear as a trophy
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous lie Jeff told her about a hawk
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist detail - the hawk lie is both specific and ridiculous enough to be memorable

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: A hawk came through my window and grabbed them right out of my hands. I've been keeping them as a reminder to always close my blinds.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: That's the dumbest thing I've ever heard.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Well, it happened!

jokes.text: You exploited me and made me believe in a slightly more magical world.
jokes.setup: Being angry about Jeff's deception
jokes.punchline: The oddly wistful complaint about lost magic
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment - Britta's anger mixed with genuine disappointment about lost whimsy

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: You know what the worst part is? I actually believed in you. I thought you were different. But you're just like every other guy who's lied to me.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Britta, come on—

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: No. You had something special. You had this... magic. And you just threw it away for what? A quick con? Now every time I look at you, I'm just going to see the lie.

jokes.text: If you want to collect women's underwear, can't you just buy them? / They have to be won in battle.
jokes.setup: Troy's innocent question about Jeff's trophy collecting
jokes.punchline: Jeff's dramatic warrior metaphor for casual sex
jokes.explanation: Great character contrast - Troy's innocence versus Jeff's ridiculous machismo

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Why do you have so many trophies?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: They're not trophies, Troy. They're conquests. Each one represents a battle won, a heart vanquished, a notch on my belt of romantic warfare.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: That's just... that's just sad, Jeff.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Sad? This is the spoils of victory. I'm a warrior, and these women are my fallen enemies.

jokes.text: Why do you keep bread in the freezer? And why does your bathroom mirror say, 'You're special,' when you fog it up?
jokes.setup: Admitting to snooping through Jeff's apartment
jokes.punchline: The pathetic self-affirmation mirror trick
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff character detail - the mirror affirmation is both pathetic and endearing

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I was snooping through Jeff's apartment and I found this mirror with sticky notes all over it. Each one had something like 'You're a great person' and 'You deserve success.'

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's not what that is.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Dude, it's okay. We all need a little help sometimes. I have affirmations too.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Those aren't affirmations, Troy. I wrote those to cover up the mirror because I couldn't stand looking at myself.

jokes.text: Okay! I did it! Okay? I killed Pierce! Lie. Okay, good. Just making sure.
jokes.setup: Troy's sudden confession to murder
jokes.punchline: It being a lie and Troy just testing the machine
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Troy's innocent curiosity about the polygraph technology.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I killed a man.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: What?!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I'm just testing the lie detector.

jokes.text: Silence, wench! Hi, I'm Kevin, this is Kyle, and here's a fun handshake you can do with your best friend.
jokes.setup: Troy being exposed for stealing the handshake
jokes.punchline: The generic YouTuber enthusiasm contrasted with Troy's medieval outburst
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with the contrast between Troy's drama and generic video

dialogue.speaker: Video
dialogue.line: Hey guys, what's up? So Troy Barnes has been stealing our sacred handshake. Don't forget to like and subscribe!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: THOU HAST BETRAYED ME! I SHALL SMITE THEE WHERE THOU STANDEST!

jokes.text: I forgive you, but only to escape the established pattern of self-righteous indignation followed by immediate comeuppance.
jokes.setup: The typical emotional response to betrayal
jokes.punchline: Abed analyzing and subverting the expected story pattern
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary - Abed breaking the fourth wall while staying in character

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: When someone betrays you, you're supposed to feel hurt, angry, maybe seek revenge. It's the classic three-act structure of betrayal.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: But I've watched enough TV to know that if I just accept it and move on, I become the more evolved character. So I'm going to subvert your expectations and be weirdly okay with this.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Which means you don't get the satisfaction of my pain, making you the real loser in this narrative.

jokes.text: Okay, I guess it's happening anyway. You broke my heart. Continue.
jokes.setup: Abed trying to avoid the emotional pattern
jokes.punchline: Immediately succumbing to it with deadpan resignation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - meta-awareness doesn't save him from the emotional reality

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'm not going to fall into the emotional pattern this time. I'm going to stay detached and—

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: ...I'm already doing it, aren't I?

jokes.text: You're tracking us? We're mad at you, Abed. Why? Because we already live in a totalitarian surveillance state. Do you not read my status updates?
jokes.setup: Learning Abed tracks everyone's location
jokes.punchline: Abed's casual dismissal referencing government surveillance and social media
jokes.explanation: Good Abed logic - his surveillance is minor compared to existing systems they voluntarily participate in.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Wait, you're tracking all of us? That's insane!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: It's not insane. The government does it, Facebook does it, your phone does it. I'm just being honest about it.

jokes.text: I was trying to get it back. They're expensive. And he's not moving anymore.
jokes.setup: Why Abed was lingering by Pierce's coffin
jokes.punchline: The practical consideration that Pierce won't need tracking anymore
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor with Abed's practical approach to death and surveillance

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I was just thinking... we won't need to track Pierce anymore.

jokes.text: I invented an entire language, you fliztbarping gitzgorg.
jokes.setup: Troy explaining the effects of Annie's drugs
jokes.punchline: Demonstrating the invented language with made-up insult
jokes.explanation: Great invented words that sound plausibly insulting and showcase Troy's creativity

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: She's created her own language. Watch this. Hey Abed, you're a bloggity blarghnaxian!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I know you are, but what am I?

jokes.text: I noticed whenever you were in a relationship, you hummed a lot and made pancakes in the morning. It wasn't about hurting you. I did what I did in the name of breakfast.
jokes.setup: Abed explaining his fake boyfriend deception
jokes.punchline: The purely selfish motivation disguised as sweet observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - genuine observation turned selfish but somehow endearing

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I created a fake boyfriend because I wanted to see if I could manipulate everyone's emotions by manufacturing a false narrative of personal growth.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: And it worked perfectly until you all decided to care about my actual well-being instead of just enjoying the story I was telling.

jokes.text: You're Olympic pole-vaulting hopeful Brent Underjaw?
jokes.setup: Learning about Abed's fake boyfriend scheme
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously specific fake identity he created
jokes.explanation: Good absurd specificity - 'Brent Underjaw' is a memorable fake name with an oddly specific occupation.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Wait, you created a fake boyfriend?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: He's a sommelier from Portland who does CrossFit and makes artisanal cheese.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: That's... incredibly specific.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: He also has strong opinions about craft beer and volunteers at a pottery studio on weekends.

jokes.text: Britta's the one that invited Garrett to Annie's birthday party! Troy won't sit on a toilet seat after Jeff! When we're alone, Shirley refers to you guys as 'those people.' When Annie's with other females, dude, she calls Jeff her uncle!
jokes.setup: Abed's defensive counterattack with everyone's secrets
jokes.punchline: The rapid-fire revelation of increasingly specific embarrassing details
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with specific, embarrassing, and character-revealing secrets

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Oh, you want to go there? Fine. Troy, you cry during insurance commercials. Jeff, you practice your 'serious face' in the mirror. Britta, you still sleep with a nightlight. Annie, you alphabetize your highlighters by shade. Shirley, you've named all your cleaning supplies. And Pierce... well, Pierce, you Google yourself every morning and read the comments out loud to your cat.

jokes.text: I didn't just masturbate in the study room. I masturbated everywhere. Everywhere!
jokes.setup: Chang confessing to his earlier polygraph question
jokes.punchline: The horrifying escalation of his confession
jokes.explanation: Gross-out escalation that's more disturbing than funny, typical Chang boundary-crossing.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. I lied on the polygraph. I'm not actually a real changlourious being.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: I'm not even a real person. I'm a sentient pile of fears and insecurities held together by a desire to seem cool.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Also, I've been stealing from the vending machines.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: And I may have faked my entire academic record.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: I don't even know what my real name is anymore.

jokes.text: I didn't just masturbate in the study room. I masturbated everywhere. Everywhere!
jokes.setup: Chang's confession about the study room
jokes.punchline: The horrifying escalation to 'everywhere' with emphasis
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Chang's earlier admission, though predictably gross

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: I've been going into the study room when no one's around.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: At first it was just in the study room.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Then it was in the hallways.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Then it was in the cafeteria.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Now it's everywhere.

jokes.text: Pierce admitted he was flawed, and he died rich.
jokes.setup: Jeff's philosophical speech about honesty
jokes.punchline: The practical conclusion that Pierce's honesty paid off financially
jokes.explanation: Good dark observation that ties the themes together with practical cynicism

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Honesty is the foundation of all meaningful relationships. When we strip away pretense and embrace truth, we create genuine human connection. It's not just morally right—it's fundamentally practical.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You know, I've been honest my whole life, and look at me—I'm loaded.

jokes.text: I'm Jeff Winger, and if I had my choice, I would rather look at myself naked than the women I sleep with.
jokes.setup: Jeff leading by example in radical honesty
jokes.punchline: The perfectly narcissistic confession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff character revelation - narcissistic but self-aware enough to admit it

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I have a confession to make. I've been lying to all of you. I'm not actually as smart as I pretend to be. I'm just really good at sounding smart. And honestly? I'm okay with that because it means you all think I'm amazing, which is what really matters.

jokes.text: I'm the one who hit Jeff's Lexus in the parking lot. It wasn't a taco truck, and it was racist of me to improvise such an ethnically specific vehicle.
jokes.setup: Shirley's confession about the car accident
jokes.punchline: The additional confession about racist improvisation
jokes.explanation: Great Shirley moment - the self-awareness about her racism makes it funnier and more complex

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I hit a car in the parking lot and I didn't leave a note.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: But that's not even the worst part. At the improv class I took, I did a sketch where I played a stereotypical Asian character with a terrible accent.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: And everyone laughed! They thought it was hilarious! I felt so guilty I came here to confess.

jokes.text: I've never been to Legoland. I just wanted you guys to think I was cool.
jokes.setup: Troy's confession session
jokes.punchline: The pathetic lie about Legoland for coolness
jokes.explanation: Sweet Troy moment - the innocence of thinking Legoland would make him cool

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I lied about going to Legoland. I've never been to Legoland. I just said that to seem cool.

jokes.text: I can be passive-aggressive sometimes. Don't everybody disagree at once. Oh, Lord, I did it again.
jokes.setup: Shirley confessing to passive-aggression
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrating it and catching herself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke where Shirley demonstrates the exact behavior she's confessing to, then recognizes it.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I know I have a problem with passive-aggression. I'm working on it.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Here, I baked you a cake. It's not poisoned or anything. Well, it's not poisoned.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: See? I did it again.

jokes.text: When any of you chew gum, I want to punch you. You may as well have submachine guns in your mouths. It vibrates my skull.
jokes.setup: Abed's confession about pet peeves
jokes.punchline: The extreme metaphor and physical description
jokes.explanation: Good Abed sensory overload detail with vivid metaphor

jokes.text: I also leave you this liquid nitrogen cooled cylinder of my hyper-virile sperm in case your lesbian lifestyle one day wears out and you wish to raise an army of geniuses.
jokes.setup: Pierce's posthumous gift to Britta
jokes.punchline: The elaborate sperm storage and 'army of geniuses' plan
jokes.explanation: Peak Pierce absurdity - combines offensive assumptions with grandiose delusions perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Britta, in my will, I've arranged for my sperm to be preserved in perpetuity.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: What? Pierce, no!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Yes! I'm going to create an army of geniuses. Little Pierces running around, changing the world.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: That's insane. That's absolutely insane.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Is it? Or is it the only sane thing I've ever done?

jokes.text: It's the same tiara I used to wear when my mother would verbally assault me for not being a little girl.
jokes.setup: Pierce leaving Annie a tiara
jokes.punchline: The disturbing backstory of childhood trauma
jokes.explanation: Dark Pierce backstory that explains his character while being simultaneously funny and sad

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Annie, I want you to have this tiara. It belonged to my mother.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Oh Pierce, that's so sweet!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, she won it in a beauty pageant in 1952. Back then, if you didn't win, they'd publicly humiliate you. She came in second. The judges said her smile was 'too eager.' She never smiled again.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: That's... that's horrible.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I know. So I want you to have it, as a reminder that beauty pageants destroy souls.

jokes.text: To you, I leave this bottle of fine scotch so that you're less tempted to drink this cylinder of even finer sperm.
jokes.setup: Pierce's gift to Jeff
jokes.punchline: The implication that Jeff might drink the sperm
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation of the sperm gift theme with Jeff-specific insult

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I got you something special, Jeff. A bottle of the finest brandy.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Pierce, this is... wait, what is this?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: It's not just any brandy, Jeff. It's been aged for 20 years in oak barrels.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay, that sounds nice actually.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: With a special ingredient I added myself.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What kind of ingredient?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Let's just say it came from a very happy place.

jokes.text: To you, I leave this bottle of fine scotch so that you're less tempted to drink this cylinder of even finer sperm.
jokes.setup: Pierce leaving Jeff alcohol
jokes.punchline: The implication that sperm is a beverage alternative
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the sperm running gag but the drinking implication is more gross than funny.

dialogue.speaker: Stone
dialogue.line: Pierce bequeaths to Jeffrey Winger his entire collection of premium spirits and rare alcohols, totaling approximately $47,000 in value.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Wow, that's... actually really generous.

dialogue.speaker: Stone
dialogue.line: However, there is a condition. The will states: 'Only to be consumed in the company of good friends, or in moments of genuine despair.' Additionally, Pierce notes: 'And should Jeffrey ever run out, he knows where to find more of my special homemade brew.'

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: What's his special homemade brew?

dialogue.speaker: Stone
dialogue.line: It simply says here: 'My essence.'

jokes.text: Maybe it's because everyone else got one, and because it's an old man's semen, but, um, I'm kind of disappointed.
jokes.setup: Troy receiving his sperm cylinder
jokes.punchline: The honest disappointment despite the horrifying nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy innocence - feeling left out despite the disturbing gift

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wait, that's it? That's all you're giving me?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Troy, it's a sperm sample. What were you expecting?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I don't know, I just thought... I mean, I've been saving up for this moment my whole life, and it's in a cup the size of a shot glass?

jokes.text: Millions of dollars. And being a man or whatever he said.
jokes.setup: Troy explaining what he's been searching for
jokes.punchline: The honest prioritization of money over personal growth
jokes.explanation: Good character honesty with Troy's casual dismissal of the deeper meaning

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: The honest prioritization of money over personal growth

jokes.text: Cool. Cool, cool, cool. / That's a lie.
jokes.setup: Abed's typical catchphrase response to Troy leaving
jokes.punchline: The polygraph exposing his emotional devastation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of Abed's catchphrase - perfect economy and emotional impact. The polygraph catches what he's hiding.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Cool. Cool cool cool cool cool cool cool.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. I can't believe I didn't tell you yet. It was dehydration from filling up all of those cylinders.
jokes.setup: Finally revealing how Pierce died
jokes.punchline: Death by excessive masturbation for the sperm cylinders
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark callback that ties together all the sperm gifts with Pierce's actual cause of death

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Death by excessive masturbation for the sperm cylinders

jokes.text: It was dehydration from filling up all of those cylinders. I mean, it's how I want to go, but, hey, I'm a little nutty.
jokes.setup: Finally learning how Pierce died
jokes.punchline: He died from dehydration while producing sperm samples
jokes.explanation: Darkly appropriate Pierce death - killed by his own sexual obsessions and weird bequests.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Finally learning how Pierce died

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: He died from dehydration while producing sperm samples

tmdb_overview: Pierce's funeral leaves the study group with mixed feelings, especially after their late tormentor leaves one last test to reveal everyone's secrets.
insight: A funeral episode sustains 87-point momentum through 73 jokes in pure character escalation.
